I don't take as much time for questions as I used to. With a large class you can't. When we first started and had twelve or fourteen guys in a class, half of the class time would be questions. When you get ninety-five people in here, you can't do that. But I take time out to see what the student has to say. The reason I do is because sometimes the Lord will show the [student ]{.class_s36M}something he hasn't shown [me]{.class_s36M}. I've seen it happen. Sometimes the student will say something, and although I can't answer it right then, I'll go back and look it up and get it later. Sometimes the student will say something, and it won't be anything right then for which I am looking, but it will remind me of something else. So I'll get a lead on it, and [five weeks]{.class_s36M} later I can pick it up.

["Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 8). That shows that there was ignorance in their act. Acts 3:17 says they did it through ignorance. They knew they crucified a good man. They knew that. The centurion said he was a righteous man. Pilate said, ["I find no fault in him."]{.class_s36N} They knew He was a good man, but they didn't know He was ["the Lord of glory."]{.class_s36N}

As a great Christian preacher said, many years ago: "They know not that the stars that lighted up the midnight skies were just the glittering bands wherewith He clasped the robes of His glory"

That thing was done in ignorance. When Peter got up to preach he said, ["brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did it."]{.class_s36N} That is why the Jews had a second chance. When Jesus Christ was dying on Calvary's cross, He said, ["Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do."]{.class_s36N} Well, the Lord did forgive them. That is why in Acts two, three, four, five, six, and seven they have another chance. The Lord did forgive them.

2:9 But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.

10 But God hath revealed them unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God.

11 For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.

12 Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.

13 Which things also we speak, not in the words which man's wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual.

["But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 9). In Billy Graham's column, "My Answer," written by his father-in-law most of the time, somebody wrote in and asked, "What do we know about heaven?" The writer of the column wrote back and said, "We can't know very much about heaven because it is written, ['Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.']{.class_s36N}" Then he stopped. [But you can't stop there.]{.class_s36M} Look at the next verse.

["But God hath revealed them unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 10). God [has ]{.class_s36M}revealed it to us; not by our ears, not by our eyes, not by our heart, but by His Spirit. You [can ]{.class_s36M}find what God has prepared for you if you love Him. Now, what has He prepared for you? He has a golden city, and it is 1200 miles square, and it has transparent gold apartments, ten for each person saved since Pentecost, if four billion people have been saved, working it mathematically. Then He has a body prepared for you like Christ's. He has a sinless body prepared for you in a city where you will never have to worry about sin or death or pain or suffering or sorrow or tears. He has prepared for you an eternal home in His presence, and then He is going to show you the glory He gave Jesus Christ before the foundation of the world. That is what He said He would do. So we [do ]{.class_s36M}know what He has prepared for us. ["But God hath revealed them unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}So if you want to know the deep things in the Bible, they are revealed to you by the Spirit of God. It is the Holy Spirit that shows them, [not the Greek lexicon.]{.class_s36M}

["For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 11). Now, there are [four spirits.]{.class_s36M} There is the Spirit of God, the Holy Spirit. There is the spirit of man, the human spirit. Then, Ecclesiastes 3:21 says, ["Who knoweth the spirit of man that goeth upward, and the spirit of the beast that goeth downward to the earth?"]{.class_s36N} [That's an animal spirit.]{.class_s36M} Then, there is the spirit of Satan, and that is an[ unclean spirit]{.class_s36M}. So there are four types of spirits in this universe. Man is a spiritual being. A man that tries to be happy by just feeding his body and taking care of his physical needs is doomed to failure every time. The reason is man is a spiritual being. He has bones and flesh. He has a physical body, but that isn't his [essential nature.]{.class_s36M} The reason you know that is not his essential nature is because the flesh rots and the worms eat it. Man is a spiritual being. Therefore, you will always get more benefit out of feeding your spirit than you will feeding your body.

The thing that is so interesting about this is the matter of communication. God, naturally, can communicate with anything. The devil, as ["the god of this world,"]{.class_s36N} can communicate with anything; he can reach anything. But there is a divisive line drawn between man and beast. How do you tell a dog how to fill out an income tax form? You take that [man ]{.class_s36M}and try to get that man to communicate with that [animal]{.class_s36M}, and there is a gap in there that they cannot get across. Now, he can get [some things]{.class_s36M} across.

For example, I can tell my German shepherd to come in or go out or sit up or lie down or shut up or leave or stay there or something like that, and he will understand that. With a lot of training you can teach him to jump or open doors or hunt out dope or chase burglars or find lost articles or sniff out time bombs. They are smart. You can train them to do all kinds of things like that. But anytime you think man is rated as animals are just take the simplest problem.

For example, birds by nature are [singers]{.class_s36S}. You don't have to teach any bird how to sing. Nobody has ever had to teach a bird how to [sing]{.class_s36S}. All right, if that bird is a natural singer you ought to be able to train that bird how to [sing ]{.class_s36S}easier than any other animal. You just get four birds together and try to teach them[ four part harmony]{.class_s36S} and see how long it takes you to do it. You cannot do it. But you take little boys and girls nine and ten years old, and you can teach [them ]{.class_s36S}that. There is a gap between man and animal that you cannot cross. ["For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God."]{.class_s36N}

["Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God"]{.class_s36N} (vs. 12). In plainer words, until the [new birth]{.class_s36M} you can get certain things; you can understand certain things. An unsaved man can understand that God is there. He can get [that]{.class_s36M}. God made the universe. He can get [that]{.class_s36M}. God has spoken in a Book. An unsaved man can get [that]{.class_s36M}. God is holy. He can get [that]{.class_s36M}. God is truthful. He can get [that]{.class_s36M}.[ But he cannot communicate with God until he is born again.]{.class_s36M}

You can't understand God. Even after we are saved we have some problems trying to understand Him. There are certain things that God will do in your life that you cannot understand as an unsaved person. There is no way in the world you can understand them. Even with a [saved man]{.class_s36M} there will be things coming up with which you are going to have problems. That is because you are not the [Lord]{.class_s36M}. You are a man.

I don't guess a man can understand the devil until he gets saved. There are some things about the devil, even [after you get saved,]{.class_s36M} that you won't understand. I don't understand why the devil doesn't quit. That's what I don't understand. If I was the devil and knew the Bible like the devil knows it (and don't you worry, he knows it. There's not a line in there he doesn't know),[ I would just quit.]{.class_s36M} There is something there, that isn't revealed in the Scripture, that keeps him going. Why doesn't he just say, "Well, I won't be the antichrist." Doesn't the Bible say he is going to be the antichrist incarnate? Well, why doesn't he just say, "I just won't come. I'll just sit up here in Jupiter another fifty thousand years and mess up the Bible." God has some kind of bridle or hook on the devil that isn't [revealed ]{.class_s36M}in Scripture. But just think, if the devil is that powerful, then just how much more powerful must the Lord be,[ if he can control the devil.]{.class_s36M} And He controls him.

["For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him?"]{.class_s36N} (vs. 11). Notice the small 's' in that passage for the spirit of man, which matches the spirit over in 1 Corinthians 14:2 on tongues, ["howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries."]{.class_s36N} ["Even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God" ]{.class_s36N}(vs. 11).

["Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 12). Notice how the Holy Spirit there is referred to with a small "s," talking about the attitude of the things the Holy Spirit brings; not the person of the Holy Ghost, but His work in us.

It is perfectly all right when you are talking about the work of the Holy Spirit, instead of His person, to use the neuter. In Romans 8, notice how this thing comes up again. Romans 8:16, ["The Spirit" (]{.class_s36N}capital S here). ["The Spirit itself,"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}instead of Himself. ["The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}So the Holy Spirit is sometimes referred to as "it" instead of "him" and sometimes has a small "s" when referring to His work. After all, the Spirit of God is not just a person but an influence, as all spirits are. Exodus 35:31, ["And he hath filled him with the spirit of God."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Small "s."

A spirit is an [influence]{.class_s36S}, and in that respect the Holy Spirit isn't any different from any other kind of spirit. Now, if you are talking about the Holy Spirit as the[ third person]{.class_s36S} of the Trinity in the Godhead, you say, ["Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you\....for he shall not speak of himself"]{.class_s36N} (John 16:13). When you talk about the Holy Spirit just as a spirit, what He does, you could use small "s," or you can use the neuter. And so it is used here, because the Holy Spirit is an influence. Now, don't reduce it to a Campbellite, teaching. A Campbellite believes the Holy Spirit is [just ]{.class_s36S}an influence. You won't find a Campbellite in this world that believes in the Deity of the Holy Spirit. With the Campbellite He is just an influence. But He is both. He is a person and an influence. The Holy Spirit is called the anointing. That is neuter, too.

["Which things also we speak, not in the words which man's wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 13). The words with which the Holy Ghost teaches are the words of Scripture. That is not some unknown mumbo jumbo talking in tongues. That verse says, ["but which the Holy Ghost teacheth."]{.class_s36N} Well, the Holy Ghost has written His words down, and He has got a Book full of them. ["Comparing spiritual things with spiritual."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That is how you learn. You learn the Bible by ["comparing spiritual things with spiritual."]{.class_s36N}

2:14 But the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.

15 But he that is spiritual judgeth all things, yet he himself is judged of no man.

16 For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

["But the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 14). An unsaved man cannot get them.

Now a great verse to memorize.

["But he that is spiritual"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 15). A man, who has received the Holy Spirit, ["judgeth all things."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Nothing [wrong ]{.class_s36M}with judging. The Bible says, ["Judge not, that ye be not judged,"]{.class_s36N} but that is talking about people. This verse is talking about [things]{.class_s36M}[.]{.class_s36P-0}[ "But he that is spiritual judgeth all things, yet he himself is judged of no man."]{.class_s36N}

Here is an example of judging things and not judging person: If a fellow does something that you don't think is right, you judge what he does as not right. Don't judge him. You can't say, "Well, now, if that fellow had been saved, he wouldn't have done that." You don't [know ]{.class_s36S}that.

["Which things also we speak"]{.class_s36N}[---s]{.class_s36S}piritual things.

["With spiritual."]{.class_s36N} Now, the last ["spiritual"]{.class_s36N} thing doesn't say spiritual [what]{.class_s36S}, but looking that verse over and comparing every part of it, it looks like it is [words]{.class_s36S}. Look at this thing in verse four,[ "words"]{.class_s36N}; verse six, ["speak"]{.class_s36N}; verse seven, ["speak."]{.class_s36N} It looks like when he said ["comparing spiritual things with spiritual,"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36S}he means the things that God has shown us we should compare with the [words ]{.class_s36S}God has given us and we try to make the things God has given us match the [words]{.class_s36S}. That is what it looks like. It looks like the last ["spiritual"]{.class_s36N} is a reference to spiritual words. ["Which things also we speak, not in the words which man's wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36S}That is, comparing the things which God has shown us with the word of God, spiritual [words]{.class_s36S}, not the words of this world.

["Comparing spiritual things."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Like the first of the verse, ["Which things\...comparing spiritual things."]{.class_s36N}

The next thing in verse fifteen I noticed was this. Evidently the context of ["But he that is spiritual judgeth all things"]{.class_s36N} is spiritual [things]{.class_s36M}, verse 13. ["Yet he himself is judged of no man"]{.class_s36N} is like verse 14 where no natural man can draw a judgment on spiritual things because ["they are foolishness unto him."]{.class_s36N} So it looks like ["But he that is spiritual judgeth all things]{.class_s36N}["]{.class_s36P-0} refers to the fact that a saved man has the power to discern spiritual things, and a natural man cannot judge him in those things because the natural man can't get them. ["Yet he himself is judged of no man"]{.class_s36N}; that is,[ ]{.class_s36M}no [spiritual ]{.class_s36M}man.

That's probably it. I couldn't prove it for sure, but I went through about eight translations and sixteen commentaries. My method in those things is I get everything together, and if I can get any help from anywhere that won't contradict the [King James Bible]{.class_s36S}, I'll take it. But I will not change the [King James' ]{.class_s36S}text. The [King James']{.class_s36S} text will have to stand. If they disagree with that, they have got the wrong interpretation.

["For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 16). When you get saved, the Holy Spirit comes in you and leads you to think like Jesus Christ thinks. Although you [have ]{.class_s36M}the mind of Christ, you may not always [use ]{.class_s36M}the mind of Christ. Romans 12:2 says, ["Be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Philippians 2:5 says, ["Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}When you look at a thing and decide whether it is right or wrong (["he that is spiritual judgeth all things"]{.class_s36N}) and have to draw a judgment on something that takes place, the Lord has equipped you to see that thing [the way He sees it.]{.class_s36M} You may not look at it that way, and you may not choose to, but that is only if you yield to [your own mind ]{.class_s36M}and your own imagination and are not transformed to [His.]{.class_s36M}

You, as a Christian, should pray that you will listen to Christ's mind in you. Your mind has thoughts, and you need to pray that you will listen to the right ones. I hear testimonies from Christians who were on dope before they were saved, and they say, "I got my head back together." Well, if you have been on dope, you have a disadvantage, because there is a real sense there in which the brain cells have been damaged. The mind has been affected. But the Lord will help you get it back together. ["For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind" ]{.class_s36N}(2 Tim. 1:7). The question is how much you will let it do the thinking for you.

In 1 Corinthians 2 we find:

What the Christian revelation is[​]{.class_s375} 2

Where the Christian revelation is from[​]{.class_s36W}10

How the Christian revelation

  should be preache[d]{.class_s36Y}             13

What our attitude toward it should be......7

[]{#c9X.xhtml}

## Chapter 3 {.heading_s39}

3:1 And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ.

2 I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able.

3 For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?

Note the three types of men in 1 Corinthians: a. the natural man (2:14) who is dead to all spiritual things, b. the carnal Christian who is a humanist, c. the spiritual Christian who is a disciple of Christ.

["And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ]{.class_s36N}["]{.class_s36P-0} (vs. 1). ["Babes"]{.class_s36N} is old English spelling for babies. A carnal Christian is a [baby]{.class_s36M}. Any man when he is first saved is a [baby]{.class_s36M}. He hasn't grown up. So it stands to reason that the longer a man is saved the more spiritual he ought to become. Unfortunately, that doesn't happen. I wish it did happen.

You know, when I got saved I got some terrible shocks. The night I got saved (1949), I came down and shook hands with a bunch of folks in front of the church. They were singing, "Bless be the tie that binds our hearts in Christian love. The fellowship of kindred minds\...." I was standing there, and I thought I was shaking the hands of angels. I thought I was already dead and gone home to heaven. If you had told me some of the stuff those people were doing, I would have probably renounced my Christianity. I had to learn.

I remember Glen Schunk told me right after I got saved, "You are going to find that the closer you get to God, the closer you will get to the devil." I didn't believe that then. I believe it [now]{.class_s36M}. He said, "And you are going to find the people who hurt you most in Christian service are going to be other Christians." I didn't believe him then. [I believe him now.]{.class_s36M}

When I got saved I thought, "Man, if a fellow has been saved thirty years, he will have a white beard, flashing eyes, speak with the voice of a prophet, be filled with the Spirit, call down fire from heaven, and walk around with a Bible under his arm." I thought he would be a real saint like Elijah and Moses. Boy, let me tell you, when I met some Christians that had been saved for thirty years (especially some of the deacons in Southern Baptist churches), I didn't know where I was, man! I mean, they weren't like angels. They were like [buzzards]{.class_s36M}. I thought if a fellow had been saved thirty years and had thirty years to grow, he would be like a saint. That really shocked me. It is a sad fact, but sometimes a fellow who gets in the word right after he is saved is more mature than some Christians when they have been saved thirty years.

I've met Christians who have been saved twenty years who are just like babies. They can't control their temper. They get all upset and tore up and have a fit about somebody calling them "names." Some of them get mad and storm out of a business meeting and don't go back to church for thirty years. They're babies. You ask them to quote a verse of Scripture, and they can't even quote John 3:16. They are like those two tramps who came to dinner, and their hostess said, "Let's each quote a verse of Scripture before we eat." Those tramps looked at each other, and when it came their turn, one of them said, ["Jesus wept."]{.class_s36N} The other one said, "Yeah, He sure did." I think that is all the Scripture some Christians know. By the time you have been saved five years you ought to know at least five verses of Scripture. Surely one a year isn't too much. Why, you take some of these Southern Baptists. They could quote all the Scripture they know in fifteen minutes.

Now, your growth depends upon the word. ["Desire the sincere milk of the word, that ye may GROW thereby"]{.class_s36N} (1 Pet. 2:2). ["I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(Acts 20:32). ["Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(Matt. 4:4). So your [growing ]{.class_s36M}depends upon the time you spend with the Book. If you are a baby and you are saved, you are carnal. In a minute, we will see what a carnal Christian is.

Notice when a baby starts, look how he has to feed. ["I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}\[up until now\] [ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 2). Some of the word of God is like [meat]{.class_s36M}, some of it is like [bread]{.class_s36M}, some of it is like [apples]{.class_s36M}, some of it is like [honey]{.class_s36M}. A baby doesn't eat meat. He doesn't eat bread. Do you know what he eats? He drinks milk. You put a little syrup in to make it sweet. That's the honey coming in. Then you dip bread in and "sop it."

It is a humiliating thought to think, but some of you grown men are just babies. Did you know the longer a fellow waits to get saved the more of a baby he is when he gets saved? When you are saved after twenty years old, you have had time to form habits and ways of thinking that you are not going to get over in two or three weeks. There is a beautiful advantage to getting saved when you are eight, nine, or ten years old. You don't have anything to [unlearn]{.class_s36M}.

You may make a mess of your life. I know a lot of Christians that got saved at eight or nine-years-old and were raised in a Christian school and still made a mess of their life. Just because you were raised right and had all the chances does not mean you are going to be a success. You can "blow it" too. But you have an advantage in that you don't have as much to unlearn.

You get saved at twenty years or older, and you have got some things that you have got to [unlearn]{.class_s36M}. You get saved after thirty years old, and you've got some more. By the time you get forty years old you have got so much to [unlearn ]{.class_s36M}that it is awfully hard to grow. [Growing is changing. ]{.class_s36M}And it is awfully hard to change after you are thirty or forty years old. After you are fifty, it is just downright near impossible. So the best time to get saved is when you are young.

When a fellow first gets saved, then, he is a baby. So you have to feed him milk. At Pensacola Bible Institute, we have people who have read Clarence Larkin. We've got people who have read the Scofield notes. We probably have people who have read material by Stam and Bullinger. That is advanced. That is way out---deep. There are all kinds of people in PBI. You have got babes, just saved; some are growing; some have been growing a long time. Don't get discouraged, just because you've only been saved four or five years, that you can't mature. [Your maturity depends upon the time you spend with the Lord and His word]{.class_s36M}. You can grow as much in a year as some Christians grow in ten years. I've seen it. I've seen Christians, in [three years, ]{.class_s36M}mature and become a more mature Christian in three years than some of the brethren did in[ fifteen years.]{.class_s36M}

Here is the mark of the carnal Christian. Notice, please, it is not smoking, it is not drinking, it is not going to movies. These Pharisees try to make you think the [carnal ]{.class_s36M}Christian is a [worldly ]{.class_s36M}Christian. A worldly Christian is a backslidden Christian. But did you know that a fellow can be backslidden and be [spiritual]{.class_s36M}? Did you know that? There are Christians, right now, that have turned their back on the Lord because they got mad at Him or got discouraged or disappointed. They are out there in the world trying to be happy, and they are just as miserable as a dead dog. Do you know what they are doing out there? They are out there just sweating blood. They know that nothing around them is right, and they know they are not where they ought to be, and they know what's right, and they know that Book, and they know they are going to heaven. But some of them are so miserable they wish they could die and get on home to heaven. They're spiritual, but they are backslidden.

Now, what is a [carnal ]{.class_s36M}Christian?

["For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying"]{.class_s36N} (vs. 3). Got it? That is the first mark of a carnal Christian: [envying]{.class_s36M}. That may sound like new doctrine to you, but you are reading it, aren't you? You have your Bible open on the table, don't you? OK, look at it. There it is: ["Envying, and strife."]{.class_s36N} Christians fighting among each other, fighting among themselves: is a mark of [carnality]{.class_s36M}. ["And divisions."]{.class_s36N} Splitting up over doctrines, splitting over things that don't make any difference. Water baptism is a good one. Here is a hyper-dispensationalist striving with a guy, "You shouldn't have been baptized." What a carnal rascal. ["Strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?"]{.class_s36N} That is a carnal Christian.

["For while one saith, I am of Paul; and another, I am of Apollos; are ye not carnal? Who then is Paul"]{.class_s36N} (vss. 4--5). Who then is Bob Jones? Who then is Lee Roberson? And who is Charlie Fuller? Who is Dr. DeHann? Who is Bob Gray? Who is Peter Ruckman anyway? ["Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Who then are all these men? ["But ministers by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man?"]{.class_s36N}

["I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 6). Out there in the fields some fellows plow, some fellows plant, some fellows water, and some fellows harvest, but God gives the "increase." When you get out and start preaching, sometimes you will have to [plow]{.class_s36M}. It is no fun. I know, because most of my ministry is plowing. I get to harvest every now and then, just a little bit.

I got a telephone call one time from Jim Modlish, Rochester, New York, who wanted me in for a second meeting. Last time I was up there, we had 123 people saved in three days, and over half of them were over thirty years old. It was not by a bus ministry. Over one-fourth of them were over forty years old. They were Italian, Polish-Italian, Polish Catholics, and Italian Catholics. We had us a "blast." Do you know what that guy told me? He said he preached a funeral, and fifty-five people got saved at the funeral. I said, "Man, you are having a revival up there." He said, "Boy, it is exciting." I said, "Yeah, I guess it is." I don't remember reading anything like that in the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_s36M}. Modlish was having a revival. He was not bussing in eight thousand to eat a hot dog or hamburger. There were Italians and Polish Catholics going down that aisle, forty and fifty years old. I don't know what I would do if I ever got in a thing like that. I'd start fasting and stay on my face. I don't believe I'd look up to see what my family was doing, I would get so taken up in that thing. That year Modlish was where the wind was blowing, man. I'd like to get in on something like that! I get in them once in a while, but not often.

Most of my ministry is [plowing]{.class_s36M}. The Lord has all different kinds. I'm a base fellow. I'm down on the bottom. I'm at the bottom of the pile, always have been; perfectly content. That's where the Lord called me. God takes the "base things" and brings to nought the things that are. So I'm not going to worry about that. Right on the bottom, my job has mainly been [stumping]{.class_s36M}. Do you know what stumping is? Dynamiting stumps, rooting, and blasting. That's most of my ministry. You'll do some of that. You think it is all going to be harvest? [It won't all be harvest.]{.class_s36M} You'll get in some places that there will be so many rocks in that field that you couldn't grow dandelions. Some of your ministry will be plowing.

Some of your ministry will be planting. There will be some times that you will go into a nice field that is already plowed, harrowed, disked, and fertilized; and you will just go through and throw out the seed, but you won't see it spring up. You leave and some other preacher harvests it later.

Then you will come into some field, where the stuff is planted and isn't growing good. Do you know what you have to do? You have got to get on your knees back in that prayer room. I have been in seven churches where I was the only man praying before the service in the church. The pastor never showed up. The choir director never showed up. No deacons showed up. No member showed up. When that happens you go back in the room by the baptistery and kneel down and just pray by [yourself]{.class_s36M}. There is nobody back there but you. It is discouraging.

I remember in the early years of my ministry, I was at a church in the "boonies," and things weren't right in my home, and things weren't right in the offerings, and I was sick, and nobody was getting saved, and no one was getting "rededicated." Sometimes when it rains it pours. I was kneeling back there with cobwebs in that Sunday school room. Nobody had been in there; there were ten-year-old Southern Baptist magazines lying around; dust all over the place. I was back there just ready to quit and go home when the folks came in the auditorium and began to sing. They had some bearded elders in the choir of that church, and they were singing, "Farther along we'll know all about it, farther along we'll understand why. Cheer up my brother, walk in the sunlight, We'll understand it all by and by." I got up and went on. Now, you get in a place like that, and you [water ]{.class_s36M}the crops. When you water the crops, you pray, but God "gives the increase."

Turn to John and let me show you something on this in chapter four. Sometimes when you go out you will harvest. Sometimes you will go out there, where some pastor has already labored for you. He has already plowed, he has already planted, he has already watered, he has already fertilized, he has already weeded the place and hoed it, and then [you ]{.class_s36M}harvest. John 4:35-38, ["Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest. And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together. And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth. I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours."]{.class_s36N}

So Paul says, ["We are labourers together"]{.class_s36N} (1 Cor. 3:9). We're in a union. I never could understand why you have apprentice Christians and journeyman Christians and master Christians. Did God intend that there be the three kinds in the union? Did God intend that there be apprentice Christians and journeyman Christians and master Christians? Well, if a Christian is an apprentice Christian, maybe he ought to be for a while, but then he should become a [journeyman]{.class_s36M}. If a guy is an apprentice electrician for thirty-five years, you know that something is wrong somewhere. The Lord intends for them to be [masters ]{.class_s36M}after a while.

["So then neither is he that planteth any thing"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 7). Is that clear? He that plants isn't anything; he's nothing. A fellow said the "ian" on Christian means "[ ]{.class_s36M}I am nothing." [Christ ]{.class_s36M}is all; "i-a-n"---I am nothing. Up north, they have the signs out that say, "The church where everybody is somebody." You should have a sign that says, "The church where everybody is nobody." Amen, brother! ["Neither he that watereth."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Nothing. ["But God that giveth the increase."]{.class_s36N} We are just sinners saved by grace. We're expendable. We are a zero. 0+0=1, if Jesus Christ is the "ONE." .0001 puts Him behind and 1,000 puts Him ahead.

["Now he that planteth and he that watereth are one"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 8). In Christ, they are one in one body. ["And every man."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Underline this in your Bible; live by it and die by it. Hang it on your living room wall, put it in your bathroom over your sink, and put it in your bedroom where you can see it. ["Every man shall receive his own reward according to his own labour."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}See that? The companion verse to that says, ["So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(Rom. 14:12). You will get what you put into Christian work. What you put in, you will get out. If you want to get something out of it, then labour; get working for the Lord. You will get in what you put out.

You hear me talking about these big churches sometimes, and I don't mean to be derogatory. I kind of make fun of them once in a while, like they will at me. But seriously, I don't know of any [big ]{.class_s36M}pastor in any [big ]{.class_s36M}church in America that doesn't work like a dog. Those fellows labor. They really do. You can talk all you want, and fellows criticize their bus ministry and their promotion, but those fellows [work]{.class_s36M}. You don't go fishing doing that kind of work. Harold Henniger had a couple of heart attacks and had open heart surgery. Dallas Billington had three heart attacks, and the fourth one took him away. Brother Rawlings got ulcers and almost had a nervous breakdown. Bob Gray had an ulcer from a four-million-dollar bond program. They work, boy. They [sweat]{.class_s36M}. They sign papers. They stay up at night. They get busses repaired. They make out those forms. They have fifty, one hundred, two hundred, five hundred workers on whom to check. Before you look at those fellows too hard, get in their position and see how much you've got to put out. You [get ]{.class_s36M}according to your own labor.

3:9 For we are labourers together with God: ye are God's husbandry, ye are God's building.

10 According to the grace of God which is given unto me, as a wise masterbuilder, I have laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon.

["For we are labourers together with God: ye are God's husbandry, ye are God's building."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}So laboring for Christ is like two things. Not only is it like growing crops (vss. 6--7), but it is also like husbandry: taking care of a vine or taking care of a flock. ["Ye are God's building."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}It's like carpentry.

["According to the grace of God which is given unto me, as a wise masterbuilder"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 10). So in the union they call them apprentice, journeyman, and master. ["I have laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}All right, three figures of the ministry: a man that takes care of crops, a man that takes care of cattle, and a man that builds a building. Those are the different pictures of the ministry in that passage. A little bit later he will give you some more figures, but those are good figures of the ministry.

3:11 For other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ.

12 Now if any man build upon this foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, stubble;

13 Every man's work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man's work of what sort it is.

14 If any man's work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward.

15 If any man's work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself shall be saved; yet so as by fire.

Here is a great passage that deals with the Judgment Seat of Christ. The Judgment Seat of Christ, when you get right down to it, is the most important doctrine in the Bible for a Christian. The Second Coming of Christ is mentioned more times, but for you practically, the Second Coming of Christ amounts to judgment. Praise the Lord for the Rapture, but the Second Coming of Christ for you is judgment. So the Christian who keeps the Judgment Seat of Christ in mind, all the time during his life as a Christian, will always wind up doing more for the Lord than the Christian who doesn't. If you keep constantly in mind before yourself day and night, "I'm going to be judged, I'm going to be judged, I'm going to be judged," you will get ten times as much done for the Lord and stay out of ten times as much trouble as otherwise you would. We call it "the long look." Some people are farsighted and some people are nearsighted. Every Christian ought to be far-sighted. When you work during the day time, whatever you do, you ought to keep in mind the Judgment Seat of Christ. That is where you are heading. You take that out, and you have cut off the incentive for living the life a Christian should live.

Before we start in verse eleven, let's go to 2 Corinthians 5:10 and Romans 14:10. Romans 14:10 has been changed in all the new Bibles to "the judgment seat of God." There isn't any such thing as a judgment seat of God. There is a [White Throne Judgment,]{.class_s36M} but there is no such thing as a judgment seat of God. It is called the ["judgment seat of Christ"]{.class_s36N} in Romans 14:10, and then it says in Romans 14:11--12,[ "For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God. So then every one of us shall give account of himself]{.class_s36N} [to God."]{.class_s36N}

Personal accountability to God will keep you out of more trouble as a Christian than anything in the world. Just remember that you will give account to God you personally, individually. Second Corinthians 5:10 speaks of it again, ["For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according that he hath done, whether it be good or bad."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Without defining exactly what that is, he says in verse eleven, ["Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}So the Judgment Seat of Christ is said to be a terror. To tone that thing down and make it [positive ]{.class_s36M}and sweet for a Christian, the modern fundamentalists teach it this way: "Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord on [unsaved people,]{.class_s36M} we persuade men to get saved." And of course, that is exactly what it does [not ]{.class_s36M}say. The people whom Paul is persuading in verse eleven are [not ]{.class_s36M}unsaved people. They are [saved ]{.class_s36M}people.

["For other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 11). Then if any man is going to build a ministry, the first thing he has to lay down is Jesus Christ, the foundation. A ministry begins with Jesus Christ. If Jesus Christ is not the foundation, you can't build on it. If Jesus Christ is not the foundation of the home, the home will not stand. If Jesus Christ is not the foundation of a nation, the nation won't stand.

Heretics may profess that He is their foundation, but He is not. They are trusting all kinds of things to save them instead of HIM: sacraments, church traditions, good works, the Golden Rule, the Ten Commandments, popes, priests, etc.

["For other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That is, God laid the foundation. ["Other foundation can no man lay."]{.class_s36N} Future. ["Than that is laid."]{.class_s36N} Past. ["Which is Jesus Christ."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That is, God determined that Jesus Christ would be the foundation of [anybody's]{.class_s36M} ministry. That is determined. If you want to build, you have to lay down the same foundation that God picked, [or you don't have any ministry.]{.class_s36M}

["Now if any man build upon this foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, stubble; Every man's work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man's work of what sort it is"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vss. 12--13).

All right, the fellow is saved, he has Christ, he has the foundation. What can be built on it? ["Upon this foundation gold."]{.class_s36N} Gold throughout the Bible symbolizes Deity. All the articles of furniture in the tabernacle were covered with gold. The mercy seat was pure gold. The table of shewbread and the altar of incense were overlaid with gold. The candlesticks were pure gold. Gold is the highest thing down here. So if gold stands for the highest thing up there, it symbolizes Deity. In New Jerusalem, the Lord made streets of gold. The city is like transparent gold. So gold is the highest value down here. It stands for the highest value up there.

Your paper money is no good unless it has silver behind it. The silver is no good unless it has gold behind it. Of course, they are always kidding you about it and saying, "We're going off the gold standard." Well, if they do, they will have to come back on it. Revelation 17 says that Babylon The Great will fool around with gold. So if they get off the gold standard, they will have to get back on it. No matter how much you try to talk about the power of gold falling or diminishing or the gold standard going down, gold is still the most valuable thing there is. If you don't believe it, go to a coin shop. They say they were surprised when people didn't buy all this gold, and now the value of gold currency has come way down. It hasn't come down when you can buy a ten dollar gold piece for thirty dollars. Just go by a coin shop and look at the gold coins and see what it costs you to buy one, or the silver coins for that matter. I've seen silver half dollars lying around there from 1931, '32, '33 that they were selling for fifteen dollars. The price is up over 800 percent. So your paper is no good without silver. Your silver is no good without gold.

Gold stands for Deity. Every time you worship Jesus Christ as God, you put up gold. Every time you honor God in your life and give God the first place, you put up gold. Every time you put God first above yourself, your neighbor, and your family, you put up gold. The Bible says, ["Lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven\...For where your treasure is, there will you heart be also"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(Matt. 6:20--21). ["Treasures in heaven"]{.class_s36N} are only laid up as treasures on earth are laid down. That's the rule. Everytime you witness about Jesus Christ as though He were Deity and brag on Him as though He were God, you put up gold in heaven.

["Silver."]{.class_s36N} What is silver? Silver is the price of [redemption]{.class_s36M}. In the Old Testament, each one of those Jews had to give a shekel of silver to redeem his soul when he went into battle. Judas betrayed Jesus Christ for thirty pieces of what? Silver. Then silver pictures [redemption]{.class_s36M}. All the sockets in the tabernacle where overlaid with silver outside in the outer court. It's a picture of buying somebody with money. It's a picture of redemption. Numbers 3:47--49, ["Thou shalt even take five shekels apiece by the poll, after the shekel of the sanctuary shalt thou take them: (the shekel is twenty gerahs:) And thou shalt give the money, wherewith the odd number of them is to be redeemed, unto Aaron and to his sons. And Moses took the redemption money."]{.class_s36N} That is redemption money and a picture of somebody being redeemed. The cross reference is Leviticus 5:15. Exodus 30:13--15, ["This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary\...Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above\...The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the ]{.class_s36N}[LORD]{.class_s36Z-0}[, to make an atonement for your souls."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}In the New Testament, you are told ["Ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold, from your vain conversation received by tradition from your fathers" ]{.class_s36N}(1 Pet. 1:18). What does this mean? This means every time you tell a person [how to get saved,]{.class_s36M} you put up silver. Every time you tell them how to get saved you lay up silver [in heaven.]{.class_s36M}

["Precious stones]{.class_s36N}[."]{.class_s36P-0}[ ]{.class_s36N}What are precious stones? Precious stones are [people]{.class_s36M}. Every person you win to Christ is like a precious stone. Malachi 3:17,[ ]{.class_s36P-0}["And they shall be mine, saith the ]{.class_s36N}[LORD ]{.class_s36Z-0}[of hosts, in that day when I make up my jewels; and I will spare them, as a man spareth his own son that serveth him."]{.class_s36N} So [people]{.class_s36M}, there, are likened to precious jewels. Turn back to Zechariah 9:16, ["And the ]{.class_s36N}[LORD ]{.class_s36Z-0}[their God shall save them in that day as the flock of his people: for they shall be as the stones of a crown."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Precious stones in a crown. The Bible says[, ]{.class_s36P-0}["Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies"]{.class_s36N} (Prov. 31:10). Precious stones.

Now, there are two things you need to get on those precious stones. The first thing is in Matthew 13, where the Body of Christ is called a ["pearl of great price."]{.class_s36N} A pearl is a precious stone. The next thing you need to get is in Matthew 7:6, ["Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That doesn't mean that you are not to give the sacrament to an unsaved fellow, like the Catholic Church teaches. And that doesn't mean you are not to witness to a guy twice after he cusses you out, like they tell you out in the world. That means you are not to take [the people you lead to Jesus Christ]{.class_s36M}, which are [precious stones,]{.class_s36M} and turn them over to [unsaved male teachers and unsaved female teachers,]{.class_s36M} 2 Peter 2:1, 22. So the people you lead to Christ you are to care for. When you get them saved, you don't tell them, "Go to the church of your choice," lest they turn again and rend [you.]{.class_s36M}

Then the question comes up, once you have laid up treasure in heaven, can you lose it? The only indication that you can [lose ]{.class_s36M}rewards is found in John's second epistle, and you evidently can only lose these rewards for [one offense.]{.class_s36M} There is only one offense given. God deals with individual sins in your own life, and you will be chastened for them and punished for them here and now. But the only indication that you can [lose ]{.class_s36M}rewards is found in 2 John 7-8, ["For many deceivers are entered into the world, who confess not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an antichrist. Look to yourselves, that we lose not those things which we have wrought, but that we receive a full reward."]{.class_s36N} That implies that it is possible for a Christian to [lose ]{.class_s36M}what he could have had. But the context of that remark (in the passage only one sin is mentioned) [is going along with and cooperating with religious liberals]{.class_s36M}. So it may be when Billy Graham gets to heaven, he will find out that he had all his reward while he was on the earth, the whole thing. He had his souls, had his money, had his congregation, had his crowds, and had his results. When it comes to religious liberals, the Bible says about those fellows that you are to ["rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36P-0}Say, "Fellow, I love you, and I'm praying for you, but do you know something? If you're not born again, you'll fall into hell and make a hole so big it will take the devil six months to patch it up."

As far as those days in your life when you aren't right with God, the backsliding days you have, you will pay for them [here]{.class_s36M}, and you will pay for them at the Judgment Seat of Christ by [losing ]{.class_s36M}what you could have wrought if you had been right with the Lord in those days. But for the days put in for Him, you will get your rewards.

All right, back to twelve, ["silver, precious stones."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Those are the people you win to Christ. Old-Time Christians used to sing, "Will there be any stars, any stars in my crown at evening when the sun goeth down?" Folks said it wasn't "Scriptural." Yes, it was Scriptural. They were talking about a soulwinner's crown and ["they that turn many to righteousness"]{.class_s36N} shall shine[ "as the stars for ever."]{.class_s36N} The closest thing you have ever seen to a star is a diamond twinkling in light. Did you ever see a diamond with light shining on it? It looks just like a star. Those [stars ]{.class_s36M}in those crowns are [saved people.]{.class_s36M} An unsaved girl---going off to a dance---said to her younger sister, as she held up a fake cut-glass necklace, "Boy, am I going to have a good time tonight." Her younger sister, reading the Bible, looked up and said, "At the Judgment Seat of Christ I'm going to get a necklace that will put that one out of business," and went on reading her Bible. That's the way to do it. You'll get your rewards.

["Wood."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Wood is a [dead ]{.class_s36M}tree. ["Hay."]{.class_s36N} Hay is [dead ]{.class_s36M}grass. ["Stubble."]{.class_s36N} Stubble is [dead ]{.class_s36M}wheat. The one thing those three items have in common is they are all dead. The Bible says,[ "How much more shall the blood of Christ\...purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}What is [dead]{.class_s36M}? All right, my [body ]{.class_s36M}is dead. It's nailed to the cross. ["I am crucified with Christ"]{.class_s36N} (Gal. 2:20). The[ old man]{.class_s36M} is dead. Everything I do for [this body]{.class_s36M} is dead. That is, if I did it for myself, it does not count, because I'm [dead.]{.class_s36M} Like Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "Some Christians spend all their life running errands for a corpse." If your life is just bound up with "me and I and me," you are working [for a dead man,]{.class_s36M} and your life is a [living ]{.class_s36M}death, and you are working for a [corpse]{.class_s36M}. When you get to the Judgment Seat of Christ the whole thing is going to go up in smoke. That Bible said, ["he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them"]{.class_s36N} (2 Cor. 5:15). So the time you waste trying to take care of Number One is just down the drain. It is just soot in the smokestack and will come to absolutely nothing. So don't be over-careful "taking care of yourself."

[​]{.class_s36U}Folks say, "Well, you have to think about yourself." [No, you don't.]{.class_s36M} You have to think about the Lord. You don't have to think about yourself at all. The time you spend on [yourself ]{.class_s36M}is wasted time, because you are dead. I don't understand [all ]{.class_s36M}the implications of that, but I do know this. I know when you sit down and take out a piece of paper and write down what you have done just for the Lord and [nobody else,]{.class_s36M} the list sure gets thin. I've had people brag about me that didn't have any sense and say, "Well, you did this and that and the other thing." No, no. You won't kid me in those kind of things. Folks say, "Well, you preach." [I like to preach.]{.class_s36M} Did you ever meet a fellow who was called to preach that [didn't]{.class_s36M} enjoy preaching? I never have. Somebody said, "Well, you travel up and down the country." [I get paid to travel up and down the country. ]{.class_s36M}Those things do not count. Folks are always trying to figure their service. The stuff that goes through the Judgment Seat of Christ is the stuff you did[ just because the Lord wanted you to do it,]{.class_s36M} and you got nothing out of it. If you get out a piece of paper and write them down, brethren how many could you write down?

I've been saved fifty-two years, and as God is my witness, if I take out a sheet of paper, I couldn't write down more than ten things I've ever done in my life that I did just because they were [right ]{.class_s36M}and God wanted me to do them and all I got out of them was a [pain in the neck]{.class_s36M}---about ten in twenty-seven years. That ain't much, man. I'm ashamed to have to make that confession. There should be at least ten a year. Folks say, "You preach on the street." [I enjoy preaching on the street.]{.class_s36M} If you don't enjoy it the first time, by the tenth time you will. ["Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? \...Feed my sheep"]{.class_s36N} (John 21:16). The motive for feeding the flock is loving the Lord. The first commandment is, ["Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(Matt. 22:37).

Now, I'll tell you, brother, sister, that is just how it is. Richard Wurmbrand, staying in solitary three years, will get through the Judgment Seat of Christ. He could have welched and got out. He could have lied and got out. It is what you do for [the Lord,]{.class_s36M} because you love [the Lord,]{.class_s36M} that you don't have to do, and for which you get [nothing:]{.class_s36M} that goes through the fire. The Lord not only offers you rewards to give you incentive, He offers to knock your head off if you don't. That's right. He doesn't just leave it at that. He gives you all kinds of incentives to love Him. Tough apples. "Read 'em and weep." That's how it goes. I don't enjoy it any more than you do.

["Every man's work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man's work of what sort it is. If any man's work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vss. 13--14). So the rewards are earned rewards for works after you are saved.

["If any man's work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself shall be saved; yet so as by fire"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36S}(vs. 15). So the Christian is saved in the sense of his [ministry]{.class_s36S}, and his [testimony ]{.class_s36S}is saved, and his spirit is saved, but his [work ]{.class_s36S}goes up in smoke: ["yet so as by fire."]{.class_s36N}

Note how "saved by fire" is defined in the [AV]{.class_s36M} text in 1 Peter 3:21. In spite of the Roman Catholic's heretical blasphemy that 1 Corinthians 3:15 refers to a Christian burning in Purgatory, 1 Peter 3:21 states that "saved by WATER" means that no water touched any the saved: THEY WERE IN THE ARK.

Lot is "saved by fire": i.e., the fire never touched him.

["Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you?"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 16). A lot of Christians don't know that. They go around worrying about whether they have received the Holy Ghost "since they have believed." The Charismatic church, the Corinthian church, was worrying about the gifts and had two whole chapters written on gifts and [didn't know they had the Holy Spirit.]{.class_s36M} Very typical. First Corinthians 12 is about the gifts. First Corinthians 14 is about tongues. And they don't know the Spirit of God was in them!

["Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you?"]{.class_s36N} The devil wants to talk you out of that. If the devil can convince you the Holy Spirit is [not ]{.class_s36M}in you, he can wreck your Christian life. If you are not aware of the fact that [inside you]{.class_s36M} dwells a clean, pure Holy Spirit that hates sin and hates defilement, you will get careless about your living and thinking and everything else. So the devil is very interested in convincing you that the Holy Spirit[ is not in you.]{.class_s36M}

There are[ two groups]{.class_s36M} that Satan has raised up to teach you that. They are the [Church of Christ]{.class_s36M} and the [Charismatics]{.class_s36M}. The Church of Christ stands up and says, "You couldn't have got the Holy Ghost because you weren't baptized according to Acts 2:38, ["Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost."]{.class_s36N} So you don't have the Holy Ghost unless you are baptized according to Acts 2:38.

The second group is the Charismatics who say, "The initial evidence of the baptism of the Holy Ghost is talking in tongues, Acts two. Therefore, if you don't talk in tongues you don't have the Holy Ghost."[ Those]{.class_s36M} are the two groups raised up by the devil to talk you out of your consecration. If you ever get to messing around with [those two groups,]{.class_s36M} you will doubt your salvation, and if you doubt your salvation, how can you witness? See. [The devil wants to shut your mouth.]{.class_s36M} If you lose assurance and don't know whether [you ]{.class_s36M}are saved or not, then you will keep your mouth shut, [and the world will go to Hell.]{.class_s36M} That is what the devil is interested in.

["If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 17). There is God destroying a Christian. First Corinthians 5 is a case of that. First Corinthians 5:5, ["To deliver such an one unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Another case is Romans 8:13, ["For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}A Christian can live in such a way that he defiles the temple and God [will kill him.]{.class_s36M} But this never has anything to do with the Christian going to Hell. Romans 8:13 is written to a Christian.

["If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}It may not be a sudden destruction. It might be a slow destruction. Cigarettes are one way. That's defiling the temple of the Holy Ghost, and the guy that keeps fooling with them, God destroys. You can see it. The nervous system goes to pieces. You start fooling with dope, and it blows your mind. It sure does. Some of you guys have been off dope now for two or three years, and you don't know it, but it still shows its effect in the way some of you fellows talk. I'm not making fun of you, and I'm not saying you are hopeless. Thank God you are off the stuff, man. Thank God you are on the right track. But for some of you, it may take two, three, or four years to get rid of the flashbacks. You defile that temple, and that temple will start to fall apart. Sometimes it shows up in your brain. The mind has blanks in it. That's what dope will do for you. These guys with cigarettes keep getting thinner and thinner and thinner. Some of them are so thin that if they would drink strawberry pop, they would look like a thermometer. They just have that "wasted-away" look.

3:18 Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.

19 For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own craftiness.

20 And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.

See Psalm 94:11 and Ezekiel 11:5.

["Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 18). You want to remember that, too, when folks start thinking you are stupid and peculiar and odd. One of our young fellows got up the other night and gave his testimony in the class. He said that down where he works they call him "peculiar" and an "odd ball," and they think he is stupid because he witnesses and talks about the Lord. Well, he is just getting wise, and [they ]{.class_s36M}are stupid. The Bible says,[ "If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise."]{.class_s36N} Paul said, ["We are fools for Christ's sake"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(1 Cor. 4:10). Whose fool are you?

I don't understand folks my age. I can't get along with a lot of them. There may be twenty people my age in this town with whom I can get along. I don't understand them because they are unwilling to be a fool ["for Christ's sake."]{.class_s36N} That is what I don't understand about them. Anybody my age has made an ass out of himself on a number of occasions. Did you know that? There isn't anybody in this building that is thirty years old that hasn't been a blank fool over [something]{.class_s36M}. Amen? Amen, brother! All right, if you are going to be a fool over the [devil ]{.class_s36M}and a fool over the [flesh ]{.class_s36M}and a fool over the [world ]{.class_s36M}and a fool over [yourself ]{.class_s36M}about various things, why can't you be a fool for something worthwhile? I don't understand it. I don't understand people that get forty, forty-five, and fifty years old and then get conservative and get afraid to stick their necks out. I think [that ]{.class_s36M}would be the time to stick it out. You're getting near the end, man. If you don't have any rewards, you had better hustle!

["For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 19). That is a great statement. ["The wisdom of this world is foolishness with God."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That's philosophy: ["the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God."]{.class_s36N}

["For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own craftiness."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Intellectuals think they are wise and the Lord just messes them up. There's Einstein bragging about relativity; everything is "relative." And he dies and goes on home to Hell without hope and without God: no new birth, no Christ, no anything. There's old Freud saying your trouble is "Oedipus complex": your trouble is your mother got scared by a freight train before you were born or something; go back to your childhood when you had this "traumatic experience" and fell out of your crib and bumped into a cat and all this kind of business. That old boy got up there in age and he had nine operations on his jaw for smoking cigars. He couldn't quit smoking, and he died of cancer of the jaw. After talking to you about oral complexes and putting stuff in your mouth, he couldn't keep cigars out of his. The Lord ["taketh the wise in their own craftiness."]{.class_s36N}

["And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36S}(vs. 20). Psalm 94:11 is the quotation in verse 20, and Job 5:13 is quoted in verse 19. Paul has already said, ["the foolishness of God is wiser than men" ]{.class_s36N}(1 Cor. 1:25).

3:21 Therefore let no man glory in men. For all things are yours;

22 Whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come; all are yours;

23 And ye are Christ's; and Christ is God's.

"[For all things are yours; Whether Paul."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}He's yours.[ ]{.class_s36M}["Or Apollos."]{.class_s36N} He's yours. ["Or Cephas."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}He's yours. God gave them to minister to you. ["Or the world."]{.class_s36N} The world is yours. A little bit later he says to [use ]{.class_s36M}it, but don't [abuse ]{.class_s36M}it, (1 Cor. 7:31). ["Or life."]{.class_s36N} You've got life ["or death."]{.class_s36N} Death is yours. You've got it whipped. Romans 8:38--39: ["For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come, Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord."]{.class_s36N} All of these things are yours.

["And ye are Christ's; and Christ is God's"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 23). You've "got it made." The whole thing is yours. I get to thinking about it sometimes when I go to the Spa and nobody is in the Sauna and I go in there by myself. I think to myself, "Isn't this nice? The Lord just gave me a private Sauna."

I was in a big Howard Johnson's joint out in Tulsa, big old thing with about 500 rooms and a Sauna and a Whirlpool and an indoor pool and an outdoor pool and all this junk, you know. At night there would be nobody in that pool at all, nobody. Everybody was inside. Nobody was out in the pool. I got to swim ten laps in that pool by myself and thought to myself, "Isn't this something? I've got my own private pool here that the Lord gave me." There was nobody to bother me.

Go down to the beach and walk up and down that beach. That beach is yours. You say, "Well, I live in a little, old trailer with the cockroaches and don't have anything." Yeah, but you go out there in the woods and go through those woods, and they are [yours]{.class_s36S}. Does this world belong to God? Do you belong to [Him]{.class_s36S}? It is [yours]{.class_s36S}. The clear title deed to this world goes back to Genesis 1:1. My Father has the clear title deed.[ It's mine.]{.class_s36S} ["And ye are Christ's; and Christ is God's."]{.class_s36N}

[]{#cCT.xhtml}

## Chapter 4 {.heading_s39}

4:1 Let a man so account of us, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God.

2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful.

The[ "us"]{.class_s36N} of verse 1 refers to Paul, Apollos, and Cephas back up in 3:22.

Notice the stewardship of verses 1 and 2. A [steward ]{.class_s36M}is somebody who is given a trust or "charge." A steward on board an ocean liner and a stewardess in an airplane are given charge of the passengers, to take care of the cabin and things. A steward has been given charge of something, and he needs to be faithful in that charge given to him. In this case, verses one and two, it has nothing to do with [money]{.class_s36M}, although that is the parable on stewardship in Luke sixteen. This is stewardship of ["the mysteries of God."]{.class_s36N}

A hunter said to another unsaved friend, "I loaned my bird dog to a steward." "What is a steward?" asked his buddy. "Well, he's kinda like a deacon in a Baptist church." The buddy said, "Man, you don't know it but you just lost a bird dog."

All right, if a man is a minister, he is given [seven mysteries]{.class_s36M}, and he is to be a faithful [steward ]{.class_s36M}of those mysteries. If every minister had been a faithful steward of the [seven mysteries ]{.class_s36M}I'm going to show you, the United States would not be in the mess in which it is, and the world wouldn't be in the mess in which it is.

The first of these mysteries is 1 Timothy 3:16, the virgin birth of Jesus Christ, ["And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}That is the first mystery, ["the mystery of godliness."]{.class_s36N} The first mystery is that [God came down here as a baby. ]{.class_s36M}You can't [explain ]{.class_s36M}that. It is a mystery. You can [believe ]{.class_s36M}it, but you can't [explain ]{.class_s36M}it "for love nor money."

The second mystery is in Colossians, and this is the mystery of [how Christ can indwell the body of a believer.]{.class_s36M} This mystery is called ["Christ in you, the hope of glory."]{.class_s36N} Colossians 1:27, ["To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory."]{.class_s36N}

How can you explain Christ being [in ]{.class_s36M}a Christian? How many of you people profess that Christ is [in ]{.class_s36M}you? How do you [explain ]{.class_s36M}that? Are there hundreds of "Christs" in this nation? How is Christ in each of you readers and you and in [me ]{.class_s36M}unless He is all split up? ["Is Christ divided?"]{.class_s36N} Somebody said, "Well, He's holy." How do you explain 100 different "wholes"? You can't [explain ]{.class_s36M}it. It is a mystery.

All right, the next mystery is in Ephesians chapter five, [the mystery of the Jew and Gentile being in one body in Christ.]{.class_s36M} This is said to be ["a great mystery."]{.class_s36N} Not only is Christ in you, but the Bible says you are in Him. Ephesians 5:32, ["This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church."]{.class_s36N}

What is the mystery? Ephesians 5:30---that you are a member of Christ's body, ["For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Can you explain that? You folks that are married, you are in one seat, and your wife is in another seat. You tell me you are the same bone and the same flesh! How do you figure that? [Your bone and flesh]{.class_s36M} is one place, and [her bone and flesh]{.class_s36M} is another place. What do you mean they are one, "bone of his bone and flesh of his flesh"? Explain it. You can't explain it.[ It is a mystery.]{.class_s36M}

Where is Christ? He is up in heaven. Where are you? Down here. And you are [one]{.class_s36M}? Explain it. I would like to see you try to explain it. There is no way in the world you can explain that. How do you explain a fellow down here being part of somebody that is 150 million light years straight north? You can't explain it. [It is a mystery]{.class_s36M}.

All right, let's take the next mystery. This mystery will be[ the mystery of Israel.]{.class_s36M} Like I said, "if every minister had been faithful in propagating these mysteries and standing by them and preaching them, the world wouldn't be in the mess in which it is. America wouldn't be in the mess in which it is."

Romans 11:25, ["For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery\...that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in."]{.class_s36N}

Do you know what that means? That means someday Israel is going to get right and get saved and rule Palestine. If every minister had been faithful in that mystery you wouldn't have any [postmillennial ]{.class_s36M}or [amillennial preachers.]{.class_s36M} If every minister had been faithful in that, you wouldn't have anybody [stealing ]{.class_s36M}the promises from Israel and giving them to a [church]{.class_s36M}. But they're not [faithful]{.class_s36M}.

["Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}You may not have been faithful in a lot of things. You may not be faithful in praying with your wife and reading the Bible to your wife, although you should be. You may not be faithful to the Lord in abstaining ["from all appearance of evil,"]{.class_s36N} although you should be. You may not be faithful to the Lord in performing His will for your life all the way, although you should be. But my stars, brethren! there is no need in the world for a man to be unfaithful in the mysteries. These mysteries are truths delivered to a man to propagate and believe and teach and preach, and you can do it.

The next mystery is [the mystery of the Rapture.]{.class_s36M} First Corinthians 15:51, ["Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed."]{.class_s36N} That is the mystery of the sudden disappearance of the Body of Christ off this earth, ["In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye"]{.class_s36N} (1 Cor. 15:52).

All right, the next mystery in which the steward is to be faithful in is ["the mystery of iniquity,"]{.class_s36N} 2 Thessalonians 2:7--8, ["For the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way. And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming."]{.class_s36N}

["The mystery of iniquity" ]{.class_s36N}is ["the man of sin," "the son of perdition"]{.class_s36N}---the Antichrist.

If all preachers had been faithful in these mysteries, nobody would believe he could lose his salvation because he is one body with Christ; nobody could worry about the Holy Ghost coming [on him]{.class_s36M} because the Holy Ghost is [in him]{.class_s36M}; nobody would deny the Deity of Jesus Christ so He would be a unique religious leader; nobody would deny the restoration of Israel so the Catholic Church couldn't [steal the promises]{.class_s36M}. Finally, the people would know the world was going to end in [degeneration ]{.class_s36M}because the Antichrist is going to take over. So if the preachers had been good stewards and faithful ministers of Jesus Christ, they would have kept these things, [but they didn't.]{.class_s36M}

The last mystery is in Revelation 17:5, ["And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH." ]{.class_s36N}If the ministers had been faithful in preserving this mystery, [no Roman Catholic would ever get into political office.]{.class_s36M} Who is that woman in verse 18? Who is she, folks? I don't want to tell you! You might think I'm prejudiced! Look at Revelation 17:18; what is the woman? She's a [city]{.class_s36M}. How many mountains is she built upon? Revelation 17:9---seven. What are her colors? Revelation 17:4---["purple and scarlet."]{.class_s36N} What is her symbol? Revelation 17:4---["a golden cup."]{.class_s36N} Now, what city do you think that is? Philadelphia?!

["Let a man so account of us, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God. Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found FAITHFUL."]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}Not [successful]{.class_s36M}, you see. Faithful!

4:3 But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man's judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.

4 For I know nothing by myself; yet am I not hereby justified: but he that judgeth me is the Lord.

5 Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.

6 And these things, brethren, I have in a figure transferred to myself and to Apollos for your sakes; that ye might learn in us not to think of men above that which is written, that no one of you be puffed up for one against another.

7 For who maketh thee to differ from another? and what hast thou that thou didst not receive? now if thou didst receive it, why dost thou glory, as if thou hadst not received it?

8 Now ye are full, now ye are rich, ye have reigned as kings without us: and I would to God ye did reign, that we also might reign with you.

["But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you]{.class_s36N} (that is, in regards to whether or not he is faithful) [or of man's judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}(vs. 3). That is, we can't know for sure whether we were faithful in these things until we get home to glory.

["For I know nothing by myself; yet am I not hereby justified: but he that judgeth me is the Lord." ]{.class_s36N}You ["know nothing"]{.class_s36N} about your faithfulness unless God shows you something. Just because you [assume ]{.class_s36S}you are faithful doesn't mean that you are ["justified"]{.class_s36N} in thinking so. The Lord knows whether you are faithful or [not]{.class_s36S}, and the Lord will judge you as to whether you were faithful, [not you and not man.]{.class_s36S}

["Therefore judge nothing]{.class_s36N} (in regard to the mysteries here) [before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God."]{.class_s36N}

That's quite a thing there. That's the Judgment Seat of Christ again, and when the Lord brings ["to light the hidden things,"]{.class_s36N} every man will have praise of God in two senses. First of all, we will make God the object: ["then shall every man"]{.class_s36N} praise God, because of His judgment. Or make God the subject:[ ]{.class_s36M}["then shall every man have praise of God,"]{.class_s36N}[ ]{.class_s36M}God will praise the fellow where he has been faithful and judge him where he hasn't. It will work either way. You say, "Well, God won't praise men." But yes, He will.
